records.ety: [Cf. Prov. E. caddle to coax, spoil, fondle, and Cade, a. & v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Coddled (-d'ld); p. pr. & vb. n. Coddling (-dlng).]

defs: To parboil, or soften by boiling.
quotes: It [the guava fruit] may be coddled.

defs: To treat with excessive tenderness; to pamper.
quotes: How many of our English princes have been coddled at home by their fond papas and mammas!
He [Lord Byron] never coddled his reputation.
